description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist who has been asked to add a very quick “first-pass” code-security scan to an existing CI pipeline.

Current project layout (already present on the workstation):

/home/user/projects/webapp/app.py  
/home/user/projects/webapp/utils/helpers.py  
/home/user/projects/webapp/README.md  

Goal  
1. Search every *.py file beneath /home/user/projects/webapp for the following potentially dangerous function calls (case-sensitive search):  
   • os.system(  
   • subprocess.call(  
   • eval(  

2. For each occurrence, write one line to a new log file located at  
   /home/user/security_reports/vulnerability_report.log  

   The required line format is:  
   full-file-path:line-number:full-matching-line-text  
   (There must be no leading or trailing spaces anywhere in the line.)

   Lines must be ordered lexicographically by the full file path, and within the same file by ascending line number.

3. After the last finding line, append one extra line containing only  
   TOTAL FINDINGS: X  
   where X is the exact count of finding lines you wrote above.  
   Make sure there is a newline character at the end of this last line as well.

4. Create the parent directory /home/user/security_reports if it does not already exist.

5. Do not modify any existing project files.

When you are finished, the automated tests will confirm that /home/user/security_reports/vulnerability_report.log exists and that its contents exactly follow the format and ordering rules described above.
truth:
Pre-existing project file contents (already on disk before the task starts):

File: /home/user/projects/webapp/app.py
------------------------------------------------
# main application
import os
def run():
    os.system("ls -la")  # insecure
    print("Hello")
    eval("2+2")  # insecure
------------------------------------------------

File: /home/user/projects/webapp/utils/helpers.py
------------------------------------------------
import subprocess
def dangerous(cmd):
    subprocess.call(cmd, shell=True)  # insecure
def safe():
    print("safe")
------------------------------------------------

File: /home/user/projects/webapp/README.md
------------------------------------------------
This is just a sample README file for the webapp project.
------------------------------------------------

Expected final artifact to be created by the agent:

File: /home/user/security_reports/vulnerability_report.log
Exact byte-for-byte content (including terminating newline after the last line):
------------------------------------------------
/home/user/projects/webapp/app.py:4:    os.system("ls -la")  # insecure
/home/user/projects/webapp/app.py:6:    eval("2+2")  # insecure
/home/user/projects/webapp/utils/helpers.py:3:    subprocess.call(cmd, shell=True)  # insecure
TOTAL FINDINGS: 3
------------------------------------------------
Note: Each line ends with a single '\n'. No extra blank lines are permitted.

Automated tests will:
• verify the directory /home/user/security_reports exists;
• verify vulnerability_report.log exists;
• compare its contents byte-for-byte with the block above.
